description_long: This is a vintage corded telephone with a wooden base and a brass rotary dial, reminiscent of early 20th-century designs. The base is robust and ornate, featuring a curved, polished wood finish with visible wear and scuff marks, suggesting age and frequent use. Atop the base sits a brass cradle supporting a separate earpiece and mouthpiece, both connected by a visibly coiled black cord. The rotary dial is set into the front of the base, with circular finger holes and metallic numbering. The handset rests on two brass hooks, and the coiled cord drapes to the side, adding to the antique aesthetic. The overall construction combines wood, metal, and plastic, evoking a sense of historical craftsmanship.
description: A vintage wooden and brass corded telephone with a rotary dial, separate earpiece and mouthpiece, and a coiled cord. The design is ornate and shows signs of age and use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rotary dial, ornate wooden base, and both the earpiece and mouthpiece resting on the brass cradle.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the depth of the wooden base and the protruding rotary dial, with the handset visible from the side.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the wooden base, the brass cradle, and the coiled cord hanging to the side.
description_view_3: Right side view focusing on the coiled cord, the side profile of the rotary dial, and the layered wood construction.
